title: Abstractive vs. Extractive Summarization
concept: Extractive summarization copies key sentences directly; abstractive summarization generates new, paraphrased text to capture meaning.

exercises.prompt: When you ask someone for a "summary" of a movie, what are you looking for?

options.text: A short summary of the main points.
A word-for-word copy of the entire text.
A review rating it out of five stars.

exercises.explain: A summary is a shortened version of a longer text that contains only the main ideas.

cards.title: How Does AI Shrink Text?
cards.body: When you ask an AI to summarize a long news article, does it just copy and paste the most important sentences, or does it write a fresh paragraph from scratch? AI uses two very different techniques to make long texts short!

cards.title: Two Ways to Summarize
cards.body: **Extractive summarization** acts like a highlighter, pulling out and stitching together exact sentences from the original text. **Abstractive summarization** acts like a human writer, understanding the main ideas and generating brand-new sentences to explain them.

cards.title: The Highlighter vs. The Texter
cards.body: Imagine studying for a history test. An **extractive** summary is like highlighting sentences in your textbook with a yellow marker. You are only using the exact words already written. An **abstractive** summary is like writing a quick text message to your friend explaining the chapter in your own words.

cards.title: A Tale of Two Summaries
cards.body:
Original: "The golden retriever, which was wearing a red collar, chased the tennis ball across the green field."

• **Extractive:** "The golden retriever chased the tennis ball." (Uses exact words)
• **Abstractive:** "A dog played fetch." (Rewrites the core meaning using new words)

cards.title: Is Creative Always Better?
cards.body:
**Myth:** Abstractive summarization is always better because it's more creative.
**Fact:** Extractive summaries are often preferred when accuracy is critical (like legal or medical documents) because they cannot accidentally "hallucinate" or invent incorrect facts!

cards.title: Side-by-Side Comparison
cards.body: Let's compare how these two summarization methods handle the same document.

left.text: Copies and stitches together original sentences. 100% accurate to the source, but can feel choppy.

right.text: Regenerates the ideas in new words. Smooth and natural, but runs the risk of making things up.

exercises.prompt: What does an extractive summarizer do?

options.text: It copies key sentences directly from the text.
It translates the text into another language.
It rewrites the text using completely new words.
It adds fictional details to make it exciting.

exercises.explain: Extractive summarization pulls the most important sentences directly from the original text without changing any of the words.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An abstractive summary is made by copying and pasting the exact sentences from the original text.
exercises.explain: Abstractive summarization creates brand-new sentences and phrases to capture the meaning, rather than just copying existing ones.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank with the type of summarization that rewrites text in its own words.
exercises.sentence: When an AI explains a story using brand-new sentences, it is using ________ summarization.
exercises.explain: Abstractive summarization generates entirely new sentences to explain the original text, rather than copying it word-for-word.

exercises.prompt: Which of these summaries of the sentence: "The young dog ran joyfully through the fresh snow." is an **abstractive** summary?

options.text: "The young dog ran joyfully through the fresh snow."

exercises.explain: The first summary is abstractive because it uses the new word 'puppy' and rewrites the sentence structure. The second is extractive because it copies the original words directly. The third is unrelated.

exercises.prompt: Why might an extractive summarizer produce a choppy or robotic summary compared to an abstractive one?

options.text: They invent too many new words.
They can only copy-paste existing sentences.
They require human writers to type them out.
They are too creative and poetic.

exercises.explain: Extractive summarizers are limited because they can only copy and paste existing sentences. They cannot rewrite, shorten sentences, or generate new words to make the summary flow better.
